subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical progression from context through to conclusion
Well-structured contents page and clearly labelled sections
Focused research question aligned with investigation aims
weaknesses: Inconsistent page numbering and figure labelling
Some digressive background material reduces conciseness
Repetitive descriptive passages that add bulk without mathematical focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates good coherence and logical development with a clear structure and focused aim (Band 3). However, inconsistencies in pagination and some unnecessary narrative limit it from reaching the highest band.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (diagrams, tables, probability graph)
Generally clear logical structure linking dot-product steps to commentary
Includes precise mathematical anchors like the formal dot product formula
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (Ã vs Â·) and omitted units in several calculations
Minor ambiguities due to omitted intermediate steps and unlabeled axes
Notation errors (parentheses, vector symbols) interrupt full consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and representations are used effectively and explanations are logically ordered (Bands 3). Inconsistencies in notation and missing units prevent a Band 4 rating.

strengths: Personal data collection and choice of logistic regression show independent thinking
Clear personal approach tied to the studentâs playing position
Moderate creativity in applying vector methods to a self-generated data set
weaknesses: Reliance on an online tool for regression limits depth of engagement
Approach, while personal, lacks exceptional innovation
Further exploration of mathematical implications of creative choices is minimal
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear evidence of independent thinking and a personal approach (Band 2). The creativity and initiative are good but not to an exceptional level required for Band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on external variables and model limitations
Thoughtful analysis of probability trends and 2-D model constraints
Clear evaluation of strengths and weaknesses with reliability considerations
weaknesses: Reflection remains largely descriptive rather than critically probing
Limited discussion of alternative modelling approaches
Future improvement suggestions are brief and not fully developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates thoughtful reflection and clear evaluation (Band 2), but it lacks the depth and critical insight necessary for a Band 3 rating.

strengths: Mathematics is directly relevant and serves the research question comprehensively
Use of SL-level vector dot products and logistic regression shows strong application
Demonstrates thorough understanding with mostly correct calculations
weaknesses: Occasional coordinate inconsistencies and undocumented normalization steps
Errors in vector components and lack of justification for origin shift
Reliance on a black-box tool for regression limits demonstration of full procedural understanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant SL mathematics effectively and introduces logistic regression above the standard syllabus level (Band 5). Minor errors and gaps prevent the highest Band 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations are ordered, with each step of the dot-product calculation followed by commentary, and the probability model is linked back to the question.  Minor ambiguities remain, yet overall logical flow and clarity are good.

The student shows good understanding of vector magnitude and angle, and most numerical work is correct.  However, coordinate choices are occasionally inconsistent, and the logistic calculation relies on a black-box tool, so minor errors and gaps prevent a higher band.

The student employs diagrams of the goal geometry, annotated photographs, tables of shots and a probability graph, integrating them with equations.  These multiple representations make the mathematics clearer and are generally appropriate, meeting the âeffectiveâ descriptor.

The student moves logically from context, through measurement, calculation, probability model, to discussion and conclusion.  Although some coordinate inconsistencies and digressions are present, the narrative generally connects each section in a cause-and-effect chain, so coherence is good rather than exemplary.

Applying vector methods and a logistic model to a football context is moderately creative for an SL exploration, and organising a 50-shot data collection demonstrates initiative, albeit not to an âexceptionalâ level.

There is thoughtful analysis of why probability increases with shot number and of limitations of a 2-D model, but the reasoning stops short of probing mathematical validity or alternative modelling, placing it in the âthoughtfulâ band.

Strengths and weaknesses are explicitly listed, and impacts on reliability are noted, giving a clear evaluation; nonetheless, proposals for future refinement are brief, so the work does not reach the comprehensive standard.
The student comments on how practice, pressure and other variables might alter results, indicating meaningful reflection, yet the discussion is descriptive rather than deeply critical.

Vector notation, modulus bars and logistic formula are used, but symbols are sometimes mis-typed (|VI|, cosÎ spacing) and units are omitted in several calculations.  Appropriate language is therefore present but only partially consistent.

The task is clearly tailored to the studentâs own playing position and practice routine; section headings, photographs and commentary emphasise personal decision-making, satisfying the descriptor for a clear personal approach.

Relevant discussion dominates, yet long descriptive passages on world-wide popularity of football and repeated definitions of dot product add bulk without supporting the investigation.  Hence the work is mostly relevant but not consistently concise.
Vectors, dot product, angle calculation and logistic regression all serve the research question directly, constituting effective and relevant mathematics throughout the paper.

Choosing a personal shot, collecting original data and deciding to apply logistic regression (not covered in class) shows significant independent thinking, though the mathematical depth of that choice is limited by reliance on an online calculator.

A clear contents page, labelled sections, headings for introduction, calculations, other factors, conclusion and evaluation show purposeful structure.  Pagination and figure numbering are occasionally inconsistent, but the reader can still follow the line of thought without difficulty.

Dot-product geometry and vector magnitudes are firmly within the SL syllabus; introducing logistic regression nudges above syllabus level but is treated superficially.  Overall the mathematics used is fully commensurate with SL expectations.

annotations.comment: Expression âcosÎ¸ = a Ã b Ã· |a||b|â lacks parentheses to indicate order of operations clearly.
The Pythagorean theorem illustration in Figure 6 correctly supports the magnitude calculation.
Magnitude of V1 is not calculated in the text, leaving the normalization step incomplete.
Including the formal dot product formula in figure 2 offers a precise mathematical anchor.
The shift of origin to (22,30) lacks clear justification, making coordinate selection ambiguous.
The research question is clearly stated and directly aligns with the investigation focus on shot angle predicting goal success.
The given V1 = (18.34, 22) is incorrect; the y-coordinate should be 30 to match the shot distance.
Clarify coordinate roles and correct axis labels in vector definitions to avoid confusion.
The formula uses âÃâ instead of the standard dot âÂ·â notation for dot product, which may mislead readers.
Include intermediate expansion steps for 18.34Ã25.66 and 30Ã30 explicitly to reinforce mathematical clarity.
Consider refining the research question to specify how success and key variables (e.g. angle range) are defined to improve precision.
The contents page is well structured, providing clear navigation to each section of the exploration.
Page numbering in the contents is inconsistent with the document; verify and update to ensure accurate cross-referencing.
Descriptive statistics on global football popularity add little to the mathematical focus and could be streamlined.
Personal motivation through playing position and past achievements is strongly articulated, enhancing engagement.
Footnotes interrupt the narrative flow; consider consolidating references to maintain conciseness.
The aim is clearly expressed and focused specifically on angle and distance, providing a solid research directive.
The statistic â2.43 Â± 1.41â lacks units and context; specify goals per match and clarify source relevance.
Streamline contextual digressions to maintain focus on mathematical aims and avoid unnecessary narrative.
Figure 1 effectively depicts how lateral position affects scoring angle, aiding conceptual understanding.
Caption omits axis labels and units, and text âshowsâ is vague; add precise descriptors.
Include axis labels (metres, degrees) on figures to reinforce quantitative clarity.
The explanation of vector dot product use is well constructed and demonstrates sound mathematical strategy.
The acknowledgement of omitted vertical motion reflects awareness of model limitations.
Vector notation alternates between \overrightarrow and \vec; standardize to maintain consistency.
Verify the reliability of the blog source for dot product and cite a peer-reviewed or textbook reference.
The annotated Figure 4 clearly demonstrates shot geometry and supports visual interpretation.
Add units (metres) to vector magnitudes and include degree symbol on angle to ensure consistency.
